source_pr.title: fix a cbo plan bug when executing join distributely
source_pr.body:
when doing a join b and requring the properties that sorted by a.id, distributed join will break this property. So when the planner decides that it should keep the a's order, set the join-concurrency to 1.
@shenli @coocood @zimulala @tiancaiamao @XuHuaiYu

symptoms: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , t1 b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by a.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , t1 b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by a.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: result = tk.MustQuery("select a.c1 from t a , t1 b where a.c1 = b.c1 order by a.c1;") (executor/executor_test.go)
